revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=唐沢山神社| 所在地=栃木県佐野市富士町1409| 祭神=藤原秀郷公| 社格=別格官幣社・別表神社| 例祭=[[4月25日]]・[[10月25日]]}} '''唐沢山神社'''（からさわやまじんじゃ）は[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]の[[唐沢山]]山頂にある[[神社]]。[[藤原秀郷]]を祀る。旧[[社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]。
祭神の藤原秀郷は俵藤太（田原藤太）の別名でも知られ、[[下野国]]押領使として唐沢山に唐沢城を築城し、秀郷の子孫の[[佐野氏]]が居城した。秀郷は[[平将門]]の乱を鎮圧して[[鎮守府将軍]]となったことから忠皇の臣とされ、秀郷の後裔や佐野氏の旧臣らが中心となって秀郷を祀る神社の創建が始められ、明治16年、唐沢城の本丸跡地に創建・鎮座された。明治23年に別格官幣社に列格した。
当社より西2kmの佐野市（旧[[安蘇郡]][[田沼町]]）新吉水にある秀郷の墓所は、飛地境内となっている。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.genbu.net/data/simotuke/karasawa_title.htm 唐沢山神社]（玄松子）
[[Category:栃木県の神社|からさわやましんしや]] [[Category:別格官幣社|からさわやましんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|からさわやましんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *甲冑金具（号 避来矢）一括<ref>{{Cite web |url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== その他 === *龍綺の兜（[[佐野房綱]]ゆかりの兜、市指定文化財）

revision.text:
{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! colspan="2" class="infobox-above" style="background-color: #D3D3D3" |Karasawayama Shrine |- | colspan="2" class="infobox-image" |[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|frameless]] |- ! colspan="2" class="infobox-header" style="background-color: #D3D3D3" |Religion |- class="note" ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[List of religions and spiritual traditions|Affiliation]] | class="infobox-data" |Shinto |- ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[Deity]] | class="infobox-data" |[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] |} '''{{Interlanguage link|Karasawayama Shrine|ja|唐沢山神社}}''' is a Beppyo shrine in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of Karasawa Castle.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku kanpeisha]]

{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! {{神社 | 名称=唐沢山神社 | 画像=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | 所在地=[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]富士町1409 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=唐沢山神社}} | 祭神=[[藤原秀郷|藤原秀郷公]] | 社格=[[別格官幣社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]） | 例祭=[[4月25日]]・[[10月25日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=唐沢山神社|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''唐沢山神社'''（からさわやまじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]の[[唐沢山]]山頂にある[[神社]]。[[藤原秀郷]]を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]。 == 概要 == 祭神の藤原秀郷は俵藤太（田原藤太）の別名でも知られ、[[下野国]]押領使として唐沢山に[[唐沢山城]]を築城し、秀郷の子孫の[[佐野氏]]が居城した。秀郷は[[平将門]]の乱を鎮圧して[[鎮守府将軍]]となったことから忠皇の臣とされ、秀郷の後裔や佐野氏の旧臣らが中心となって秀郷を祀る神社の創建が始められ、[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）、唐沢山城の本丸跡地に創建・鎮座された。明治23年（[[1890年]]）に別格官幣社に列格した。 当社より西2kmの佐野市新吉水町にある秀郷の墓所は、飛地境内となっている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *甲冑金具（号 避来矢）一括{{Cite web |url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === その他 === *龍綺の兜（[[佐野房綱]]ゆかりの兜、市指定文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == 関連項目 == * [[避来矢]] * [[藤原秀郷]] == 参照資料・外部リンク == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [http://www.karasawayama.com 公式サイト] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm 避来矢の鎧] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさわやましんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の重要文化財]] [[Category:佐野市の建築物]] [[Category:佐野市の歴史]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:藤原秀郷]]

{{神社 | 名称=唐沢山神社 | 画像=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]

revision.comment: 8 revisions imported: importing all the Bekkaku Kanpeisha from simple english wikipedia which covers all of them
revision.text:
{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! Karasawayama Shrine |- | class="infobox-image" |[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|frameless]] |- class="note" ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[List of religions and spiritual traditions|Affiliation]]: [[Shinto]] |- ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[Deity]]: [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] |} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku kanpeisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]]

{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! Karasawayama Shrine |- | class="infobox-image" |[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|frameless]] |- class="note" ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[List of religions and spiritual traditions|Affiliation]]: [[Shinto]] |- ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[Deity]]: [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] |} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== {{神社 | 名称=Karasawayama Shrine | 画像=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | 所在地=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | 祭神=[[Tawara Tōta|Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | 社格=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | 創建=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | 例祭=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecrated on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Special Government Shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Dd]] [[Category:Dd]] [[Category:Gg]][[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

Karasawayama Shrine |- | class="infobox-image" |[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|frameless]] |- class="note" ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[List of religions and spiritual traditions|Affiliation]]: [[Shinto]] |- ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[Deity]]: [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] |} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== {{神社 | 名称=Karasawayama Shrine | 画像=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]

It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecrated on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Special Government Shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

{{神社 | 名称=唐沢山神社 | 画像=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | 所在地=[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]富士町1409 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=唐沢山神社}} | 祭神=[[藤原秀郷|藤原秀郷公]] | 社格=[[別格官幣社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]） | 例祭=[[4月25日]]・[[10月25日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=唐沢山神社|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''唐沢山神社'''（からさわやまじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]の[[唐沢山]]山頂にある[[神社]]。[[藤原秀郷]]を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]。 == 概要 == 祭神の藤原秀郷は俵藤太（田原藤太）の別名でも知られ、[[下野国]]押領使として唐沢山に[[唐沢山城]]を築城し、秀郷の子孫の[[佐野氏]]が居城した。秀郷は[[平将門]]の乱を鎮圧して[[鎮守府将軍]]となったことから忠皇の臣とされ、秀郷の後裔や佐野氏の旧臣らが中心となって秀郷を祀る神社の創建が始められ、[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）、唐沢山城の本丸跡地に創建・鎮座された。明治23年（[[1890年]]）に別格官幣社に列格した。 当社より西2kmの佐野市新吉水町にある秀郷の墓所は、飛地境内となっている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *甲冑金具（号 避来矢）一括{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === その他 === *龍綺の兜（[[佐野房綱]]ゆかりの兜、市指定文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == 関連項目 == * [[避来矢]] * [[藤原秀郷]] == 参照資料・外部リンク == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [http://www.karasawayama.com 公式サイト] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm 避来矢の鎧] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさわやましんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の重要文化財]] [[Category:佐野市の建築物]] [[Category:佐野市の歴史]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:藤原秀郷]]

{| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! Karasawayama Shrine |- | class="infobox-image" |[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|frameless]] |- class="note" ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[List of religions and spiritual traditions|Affiliation]]: [[Shinto]] |- ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[Deity]]: [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] |} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== {{神社 | 名称=Karasawayama Shrine | 画像=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | 所在地=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | 祭神=[[Tawara Tōta|Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | 社格=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | 創建=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | 例祭=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecrated on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Special Government Shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

The shrine was founded and consecrated on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Top level categories]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Top level categories]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia01]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecrated on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

{{神社 | 名称=唐沢山神社 | 画像=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | 所在地=[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]富士町1409 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=唐沢山神社}} | 祭神=[[藤原秀郷|藤原秀郷公]] | 社格=[[別格官幣社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]） | 例祭=[[4月25日]]・[[10月25日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=唐沢山神社|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''唐沢山神社'''（からさわやまじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]の[[唐沢山]]山頂にある[[神社]]。[[藤原秀郷]]を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]。 == 概要 == 祭神の藤原秀郷は俵藤太（田原藤太）の別名でも知られ、[[下野国]]押領使として唐沢山に[[唐沢山城]]を築城し、秀郷の子孫の[[佐野氏]]が居城した。秀郷は[[平将門]]の乱を鎮圧して[[鎮守府将軍]]となったことから忠皇の臣とされ、秀郷の後裔や佐野氏の旧臣らが中心となって秀郷を祀る神社の創建が始められ、[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）、唐沢山城の本丸跡地に創建・鎮座された。明治23年（[[1890年]]）に別格官幣社に列格した。 当社より西2kmの佐野市新吉水町にある秀郷の墓所は、飛地境内となっている。 *明治22年（[[1889年]]）10月13日、[[英照皇太后]]が行啓{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=19}}。 *明治25年（[[1892年]]）、[[小松宮彰仁親王]]が御成り{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=19}}。 *明治26年（[[1893年]]）10月17日、[[大正天皇|皇太子嘉仁親王（後の大正天皇）]]が行啓{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=19}}。 *大正9年（[[1920年]]）11月17日、山階宮家の[[山階芳麿|芳麿王]]、[[竹田宮恒徳王]]、久邇宮家の[[東伏見慈洽|邦英王]]、北白川宮家の[[北白川宮永久王|永久王]]、伏見宮家の[[伏見博英|博英王]]、朝香宮家の[[朝香孚彦|孚彦王]]、[[音羽正彦|正彦王]]が御成り{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=21}}。 *大正11年（[[1922年]]）11月14日、閑院宮家の定子女王（[[寛子女王]]のことか）、伏見宮家の[[清棲敦子|敦子女王]]、[[朝融王妃知子女王|知子女王]]、閑院宮家の[[戸田華子|華子女王]]、梨本宮家の[[広橋規子|規子女王]]、北白川宮家の[[立花美年子|美年子女王]]、[[東園佐和子|佐和子女王]]、朝香宮家の[[鍋島紀久子|紀久子女王]]が御成り{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=21}}。カエデ8本をお手植え{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=21}}。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *甲冑金具（号 避来矢）一括{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === その他 === *龍綺の兜（[[佐野房綱]]ゆかりの兜、市指定文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=唐沢山神社誌 |author=唐沢山神社々務所 |publisher= |year=1936|ref={{sfnRef|唐沢山神社誌|1936}}}} *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == 関連項目 == * [[避来矢]] * [[藤原秀郷]] == 参照資料・外部リンク == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [http://www.karasawayama.com 公式サイト] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm 避来矢の鎧] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさわやましんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の重要文化財]] {| class="infobox vcard" style="border-spacing:2px;" ! Karasawayama Shrine |- | class="infobox-image" |[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|frameless]] |- class="note" ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[List of religions and spiritual traditions|Affiliation]]: [[Shinto]] |- ! class="infobox-label" scope="row" |[[deity]]: [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] |} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Tawara Tōta|Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecrated on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecrated on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecrated on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:bump]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecrated on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Special Government Shrine]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Special Government Shrine]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Special Government Shrine. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural
