{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == Related Topics == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also! == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} [[simple:Karasawayama {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|12=draft|12=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|12=draft|12=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Karasawayama Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}}

It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural
