It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} [[simple:Karasawayama {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|12=draft|12=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|12=draft|12=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} [[simple:Karasawayama The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|12=draft|12=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former rank under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ revision.comment: 33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:唐沢山神社 up to rev 103093579
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=唐沢山神社 | 画像=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | 所在地=[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]富士町1409 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=唐沢山神社}} | 祭神=[[藤原秀郷|藤原秀郷公]] | 社格=[[別格官幣社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]） | 例祭=[[4月25日]]・[[10月25日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=唐沢山神社|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''唐沢山神社'''（からさわやまじんじゃ）は、[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]の[[唐沢山]]山頂にある[[神社]]。[[藤原秀郷]]を祀る。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]。 == 概要 == 祭神の藤原秀郷は俵藤太（田原藤太）の別名でも知られ、[[下野国]]押領使として唐沢山に[[唐沢山城]]を築城し、秀郷の子孫の[[佐野氏]]が居城した。秀郷は[[平将門]]の乱を鎮圧して[[鎮守府将軍]]となったことから忠皇の臣とされ、秀郷の後裔や佐野氏の旧臣らが中心となって秀郷を祀る神社の創建が始められ、[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）、唐沢山城の本丸跡地に創建・鎮座された。明治23年（[[1890年]]）に別格官幣社に列格した。 当社より西2kmの佐野市新吉水町にある秀郷の墓所は、飛地境内となっている。 *明治22年（[[1889年]]）10月13日、[[英照皇太后]]が行啓{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=19}}。 *明治25年（[[1892年]]）、[[小松宮彰仁親王]]が御成り{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=19}}。 *明治26年（[[1893年]]）10月17日、[[大正天皇|皇太子嘉仁親王（後の大正天皇）]]が行啓{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=19}}。 *大正9年（[[1920年]]）11月17日、山階宮家の[[山階芳麿|芳麿王]]、[[竹田宮恒徳王]]、久邇宮家の[[東伏見慈洽|邦英王]]、北白川宮家の[[北白川宮永久王|永久王]]、伏見宮家の[[伏見博英|博英王]]、朝香宮家の[[朝香孚彦|孚彦王]]、[[音羽正彦|正彦王]]が御成り{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=21}}。 *大正11年（[[1922年]]）11月14日、閑院宮家の定子女王（[[寛子女王]]のことか）、伏見宮家の[[清棲敦子|敦子女王]]、[[朝融王妃知子女王|知子女王]]、閑院宮家の[[戸田華子|華子女王]]、梨本宮家の[[広橋規子|規子女王]]、北白川宮家の[[立花美年子|美年子女王]]、[[東園佐和子|佐和子女王]]、朝香宮家の[[鍋島紀久子|紀久子女王]]が御成り{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=21}}。カエデ8本をお手植え{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=21}}。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *甲冑金具（号 避来矢）一括{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === その他 === *龍綺の兜（[[佐野房綱]]ゆかりの兜、市指定文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=唐沢山神社誌 |author=唐沢山神社々務所 |publisher= |year=1936|ref={{sfnRef|唐沢山神社誌|1936}}}} *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == 関連項目 == * [[避来矢]] * [[藤原秀郷]] == 参照資料・外部リンク == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [http://www.karasawayama.com 公式サイト] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm 避来矢の鎧] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさわやましんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の重要文化財]] [[Category:佐野市の建築物]] [[Category:佐野市の歴史]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:藤原秀郷]]

当社より西2kmの佐野市新吉水町にある秀郷の墓所は、飛地境内となっている。 *明治22年（[[1889年]]）10月13日、[[英照皇太后]]が行啓{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=19}}。 *明治25年（[[1892年]]）、[[小松宮彰仁親王]]が御成り{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=19}}。 *明治26年（[[1893年]]）10月17日、[[大正天皇|皇太子嘉仁親王（後の大正天皇）]]が行啓{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=19}}。 *大正9年（[[1920年]]）11月17日、山階宮家の[[山階芳麿|芳麿王]]、[[竹田宮恒徳王]]、久邇宮家の[[東伏見慈洽|邦英王]]、北白川宮家の[[北白川宮永久王|永久王]]、伏見宮家の[[伏見博英|博英王]]、朝香宮家の[[朝香孚彦|孚彦王]]、[[音羽正彦|正彦王]]が御成り{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=21}}。 *大正11年（[[1922年]]）11月14日、閑院宮家の定子女王（[[寛子女王]]のことか）、伏見宮家の[[清棲敦子|敦子女王]]、[[朝融王妃知子女王|知子女王]]、閑院宮家の[[戸田華子|華子女王]]、梨本宮家の[[広橋規子|規子女王]]、北白川宮家の[[立花美年子|美年子女王]]、[[東園佐和子|佐和子女王]]、朝香宮家の[[鍋島紀久子|紀久子女王]]が御成り{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=21}}。カエデ8本をお手植え{{sfn|唐沢山神社誌|1936|p=21}}。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *甲冑金具（号 避来矢）一括{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === その他 === *龍綺の兜（[[佐野房綱]]ゆかりの兜、市指定文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * {{Cite book |和書 |title=唐沢山神社誌 |author=唐沢山神社々務所 |publisher= |year=1936|ref={{sfnRef|唐沢山神社誌|1936}}}} *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、108-109頁 == 関連項目 == * [[避来矢]] * [[藤原秀郷]] == 参照資料・外部リンク == {{参照方法|date=2019年10月|section=1}} * [http://www.karasawayama.com 公式サイト] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm 避来矢の鎧] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からさわやましんしや}} [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:栃木県の重要文化財]] [[Category:佐野市の建築物]] [[Category:佐野市の歴史]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:藤原秀郷]]

{{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|12=draft|12=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|唐沢山神社|version=103093579|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} {{draft revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|唐沢山神社|version=103093579|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Karasawayama Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]]

It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|唐沢山神社|version=103093579|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple successful]][[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|唐沢山神社|version=103093579|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[Category:佐野市の建築物]] [[Category:佐野市の歴史]] Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|唐沢山神社|version=103093579|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sano]] [[Category:History of Sano]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Sano, Tochigi]] [[Category:佐野市の歴史]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki revision.comment: Text replacement - "市の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Karasawayama Shrine]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=[[File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]], [[October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|唐沢山神社|version=103093579|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[every:Karasawayama Shrine|Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Karasawayama Shrine]] [[sn:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[en:Karasawayama Shrine]] [[ja:唐沢山神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Sano, Tochigi]] [[Category:佐野の Municipal History]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Fujiwara no Hidesato]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known
