Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E}}}} | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karasawayama Shrine}} | deity=[[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]] | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]], [[:en:October 25|October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}}} '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}} It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}} ==Overview== '''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁 == See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]] == External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]. == Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha. A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }} === Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property) == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from '''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].

== Overview ==
The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>

=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

== References ==

{{Reflist}}

== Related Literature ==
{{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}}
* [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]

* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁
* [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁

== See Also ==
* [[Hiraiya]]
* [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]

== External Links ==

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].

== Overview ==
The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>

=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

{{Reflist}}

{{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}}
* [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]

* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁
* [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁

 | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]]

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|comment=no wikipedias work|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].

== Overview ==
The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>

=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

{{Reflist}}

== Related Literature ==
{{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}}
* [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]

* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁
* [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine
 | image=File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG
 | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture
 | coordinates={{Coord|36.353781|N|139.600847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity={{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|WD=Q1136623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}
 | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]]
 | established=[[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16)
 | festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]], [[:en:October 25|October 25]]
|map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates=
{{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}}
|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center
}}| prefecture={{ill|Sano|WD=Q646912}}
 }}

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].

== Overview ==
The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>

=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

== References ==

{{Reflist}}

== Related Literature ==
{{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}}
* [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁　二六興信所　1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]

* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁
* [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁

== See Also ==
* [[Hiraiya]]

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].

== Overview ==
The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>

=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁
* [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].

== Overview ==
The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>

=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

==Overview==
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|36.353781|N|139.600847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|WD=Q1136623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}} | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]], [[:en:October 25|October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}| prefecture={{ill|Sano|WD=Q646912}} }}
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂 山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁 二六興信所 1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]
* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁
== See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]
== External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|36.353781|N|139.600847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|WD=Q1136623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}} | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16) | festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]], [[:en:October 25|October 25]] |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}| prefecture={{ill|Sano|WD=Q646912}} }}
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]], was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16). In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂 山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁 二六興信所 1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]
* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁
== See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * [[:en:Fujiwara no Hidesato|Fujiwara no Hidesato]]
== External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)]
{{神道 横}}

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>

==Overview==

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|WD=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].

== Overview ==
The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|en|Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}}, was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 16). In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>

=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

* Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>

=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|36.353781|N|139.600847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|WD=Q1136623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}} | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established={{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 16) | festival={{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25}}, {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25}} |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}| prefecture={{ill|Sano|WD=Q646912}} }}
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|WD=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|en|Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}}, was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 16). In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|36.353781|N|139.600847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|WD=Q1136623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}} | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established={{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 16) | festival={{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25}}, {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25}} |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}| prefecture={{ill|Sano|WD=Q646912}} }}
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|WD=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|en|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}}, was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 16). In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂 山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁 二六興信所 1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]
* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁
== See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|WD=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}
== External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)]

A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|36.353781|N|139.600847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|WD=Q1136623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}} | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established={{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16) | festival={{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25|1=April 25}}, {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25|1=October 25}} |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}| prefecture={{ill|Sano|WD=Q646912|1=Sano, Tochigi}} }}
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|WD=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|en|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}}, was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16). In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

{{Reflist}}

Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|en|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}}, was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16). In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|en|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}}, was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16). In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.

'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|WD=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|en|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}}, was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16). In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂 山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁 二六興信所 1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]
* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁
== See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|WD=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}
== External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|36.353781|N|139.600847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|WD=Q1136623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}} | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established={{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16) | festival={{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25|1=April 25}}, {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|WD=Q2954|lt=October 25|1=October 25}} |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}| prefecture={{ill|Sano|WD=Q646912|1=Sano, Tochigi}} }}
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|WD=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|en|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}}, was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16). In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂 山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁 二六興信所 1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]
* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁
== See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|WD=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}
== External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)]
{{神道 横}}
{{shinto-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[ja:唐沢山神社]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[ja:唐沢山神社]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[ja:唐沢山神社]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Karasawayama Shrine]][[en:Karasawayama Shrine]][[ja:唐沢山神社]][[simple:Karasawayama Shrine]]
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Karasawayama Shrine | image=File:Karasawayamajinja.JPG | location=Fujicho 1409, Sano City, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|36.353781|N|139.600847|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|qid=Q1136623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1136623}} | type=[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established={{ill|1883|en|1883|qid=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16) | festival={{ill|April 25|en|April 25|qid=Q2531|lt=April 25|1=April 25}}, {{ill|October 25|en|October 25|qid=Q2954|lt=October 25|1=October 25}} |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Karasawayama Shrine|caption=|coordinates= {{coord2|36|21|13.61|N|139|36|3.05|E|}} |position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center }}| prefecture={{ill|Sano|qid=Q646912|1=Sano, Tochigi}} }}
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] in Sano, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Shrine|url=https://www.visit-tochigi.com/plan-your-trip/things-to-do/1792/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Visit Tochigi|language=en-US}}</ref> It is located on the former grounds of {{ill|Karasawa Castle|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasawa Castle|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Karasawayama Castle|url=https://jref.com/articles/karasawayama-castle.491/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=Japan Reference|language=en-GB|qq=draft}}</ref> The castle grounds including the shrine is a National historic site of Japan<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web|title=唐沢山城跡|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/232631|access-date=25 December 2019|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=ja}}</ref>
==Overview==
'''Karasawayama Shrine''' (唐沢山神社, Karasawayama Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Karasawayama in [[Sano City]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qid=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. The shrine's former Rank under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was that of a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== Overview == The enshrined deity, Fujiwara no Hidesato, also known as Tawara Tōta, was appointed as the regional governor of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} and built Karasawayama Castle on Karasawayama. His descendants, the [[Sano clan]], made the castle their residence. Hidesato, renowned for subduing the rebellion led by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|en|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|qid=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}}, was later honored as a loyal servant of the emperor. Efforts to establish a shrine to worship him began primarily by his descendants and former retainers of the Sano clan. The shrine was founded and consecration_year on the former site of Karasawayama Castle's main enclosure in {{ill|1883|en|1883|qid=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 16). In {{ill|1890|en|1890|qid=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), it was designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha.
A detached precinct of the shrine includes Hidesato's grave, located 2 km west in Shinyoshimizu-cho, Sano City.
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Armor fittings (known as Hiraiya) - A collection<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm |title=「甲冑金具 号避来矢」『とちぎの文化財』|publisher=栃木県|accessdate=2019-09-14 }}</ref>
=== Other Cultural Properties === * Dragon crest helmet (helmet associated with [[Sano Fusatsuna]], a city-designated cultural property)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == {{Citation style|date=2019th year 10月|section=1}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂 山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』86〜87頁 二六興信所 1935th year （国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）]
* 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968th year 、22頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979th year 、108-109頁
== See Also == * [[Hiraiya]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|en|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qid=Q1136623|lt=Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato}}
== External Links == * [http://www.karasawayama.com Official Website] * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/bunkazai/list/109.htm Armor fittings (Hiraiya)]
{{神道 横}}
{{shinto-stub}}
